programs.name: TennCare Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: These programs help low-income Medicare beneficiaries by paying Part B premiums and cost-sharing: QMB covers premiums, deductibles, copays; SLMB and QI cover Part B premiums. Administered via TennCare Connect for eligible seniors.[6][7]
programs.who_its_for: Medicare enrollees 65+ or disabled with low income/assets: QMB <100% FPL, SLMB <120% FPL, QI 120-135% FPL.[6][7]
programs.benefit_value: Pays Medicare Part B premiums (~$174.70/month in 2024), deductibles/copays for QMB; varies by program.

programs.name: Tennessee LIHEAP
programs.what_it_does: Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program provides grants for heating/cooling bills and crisis aid. It helps seniors maintain utility services year-round.[7]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households including seniors 60+ at or below 60% state median income.
programs.benefit_value: One-time grants $100-1,000+ depending on fuel type, household size, income.

programs.name: Tennessee Weatherization Assistance Program
programs.what_it_does: Federal Weatherization program administered in TN improves energy efficiency in low-income homes through insulation, sealing, and upgrades. It reduces energy costs for seniors long-term.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+, disabled, meeting 200% FPL or LIHEAP eligible.
programs.benefit_value: Free home improvements valued $5,000-10,000 per household.

programs.name: Tennessee SHIP
programs.what_it_does: State Health Insurance Assistance Program offers free counseling on Medicare, Medigap, and savings programs. SHIP counselors assist with applications and plan choices statewide.[7]
programs.who_its_for: All Medicare beneficiaries 65+, caregivers, family members.
programs.benefit_value: Free one-on-one counseling, application help; saves thousands via proper enrollment.

programs.name: Tennessee Meals on Wheels
programs.what_it_does: Administered through Area Agencies on Aging, delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors. Programs like those via CAC Office on Aging ensure daily nutrition support.
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors 60+ unable to prepare meals, often low-income.

programs.name: Tennessee Caregiver Respite Support
programs.what_it_does: Provided via TennCare CHOICES and AAA programs, offers temporary relief for family caregivers through in-home or facility care. Helps prevent caregiver burnout.
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of CHOICES-eligible seniors 65+ or disabled adults.
programs.benefit_value: Hours/days of paid respite care; value varies by need, up to thousands annually.

programs.name: Tennessee SCSEP
programs.what_it_does: Senior Community Service Employment Program offers part-time paid training jobs in community service. Federal program run through Tennessee Department of Labor.
programs.who_its_for: Unemployed low-income seniors 55+ unable to find employment.
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage work ~20 hrs/week; wage subsidies, training leading to employment.

programs.name: Tennessee Legal Aid for Seniors
programs.what_it_does: Legal services through Legal Aid Society of Middle TN and Highland Rim, plus Elder Law Nashville focus on Medicaid, benefits, abuse. Free representation for civil issues.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ facing housing, benefits, exploitation issues.
programs.benefit_value: Free legal services worth $5,000+ per case.

programs.name: Tennessee Long-Term Care Ombudsman
programs.what_it_does: State Ombudsman Program advocates for residents in nursing homes, assisted living. Investigates complaints, ensures quality care and rights protection.
programs.who_its_for: Residents/family in TN long-term care facilities.
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy, complaint resolution; prevents abuse/neglect.

programs.name: OPTIONS for Community Living
programs.what_it_does: A state-funded program providing home and community-based services including homemaker assistance, home-delivered meals, and personal care assistance. It allows elderly and adults with disabilities to receive support while remaining in their homes or communities.
programs.who_its_for: Elderly persons and adults with disabilities seeking to age in place with home and community-based services
programs.whats_unique: State-funded alternative to institutional care, administered through local Area Agencies on Aging and Disability across Tennessee

programs.name: Tennessee State Health Insurance Assistance Program (TN SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: Assists seniors and people with disabilities with Medicare questions and concerns while protecting them from healthcare fraud, waste, and abuse. Works in conjunction with Senior Medical Patrol (SMP).[7]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors and people with disabilities with Medicare-related questions
programs.whats_unique: State program integrated with federal Medicare assistance efforts
